summary: Iter10 — Tested deep bolt.diy integration in /builder/{id}. Backend persistence (NEW /api/v1/builder/chat/{project_id} GET/POST/PUT/DELETE) is 100% PASS (5/5 tests) and bolt-proxy isolation regression is 100% PASS (2/2: root + ?headless=1 both 200 with COEP credentialless + COOP same-origin). Frontend layout is correct: header (Workspace back link, NXT1·BUILDER wordmark, bell with count=3, New tab link), data-testid='builder-chat-panel' on left with empty state 'GOOD MORNING, ADMIN. / What will you build today?', composer textarea with correct placeholder + send + attach buttons + 'CLAUDE SONNET 4 · LOCKED' caption, data-testid='builder-workbench-panel' with iframe[data-testid='builder-iframe'] src='/api/bolt-engine/?headless=1&project=...'. COI service worker DOES kick in after first reload — window.crossOriginIsolated=true on BOTH parent and iframe after a reload (so terminal Worker spawn prerequisite is satisfied). Inside the iframe: document.documentElement.dataset.nxt1Headless='1' (root.tsx flip works), body bg=rgba(0,0,0,0) (transparent — the headless SCSS block applied). HOWEVER — CRITICAL: bolt.diy itself never finishes booting inside the iframe (it boots fine when opened standalone at /api/bolt-engine/?headless=1, where the Workbench with Code/Diff/Preview + Files sidebar + Bolt Terminal all render). Inside the iframe many module asset fetches return 403, so [data-nxt1-bolt-header] never gets created, #intro never gets created, no .workbench DOM ever mounts, and most importantly window.__nxt1BoltBridge is NEVER installed (typeof === 'undefined' even after 25s). That cascades to bridgeReady never firing in BuilderPage, which keeps the composer textarea disabled forever (Playwright observed disabled=true after 30s wait) and keeps the empty state stuck on 'BOOTING ENGINE…'. End result: the user cannot type a prompt — the entire NXT1→bolt bridge is dead inside the integrated builder. Workspace/Social/Studio/Memory/Leads pages still load with no error banners (no regression from BuilderPage rewrite). Landing page floating chat bubble still renders.

ui_bugs.issue: Textarea[data-testid='builder-composer-input'] stays permanently disabled because bridgeReady never becomes true — the bolt iframe fails to install window.__nxt1BoltBridge. User cannot type any prompt in the integrated builder.

integration_issues.issue: Iframe initial HTML loads (data-nxt1-headless='1' is set, body transparent, COEP credentialless headers present, COI=true after reload) but the iframe's module JS / app code never executes. Many '403' resource-load errors inside the iframe. Concretely: window.__nxt1BoltBridge stays undefined, [data-nxt1-bolt-header] doesn't exist, #intro doesn't exist, no '.workbench' DOM is created. When the same URL /api/bolt-engine/?headless=1&project=... is opened DIRECTLY (not inside an iframe) the bolt app boots normally: Workbench with Code/Diff/Preview tabs + Files sidebar + Bolt Terminal all render. So the failure is iframe-context specific. Most likely cause: the iframe has `credentialless='true'` AND once the COI service worker is active the parent is COI=true with COEP=credentialless — sub-resource fetches inside a credentialless iframe drop credentials, and the FastAPI bolt-engine proxy (or the COI SW itself) is responding 403 to those un-credentialed requests for the JS module chunks. Try one of: (a) remove `credentialless='true'` from the iframe attribute (parent is already credentialless via COI SW so the iframe doesn't need to opt in again), (b) make /api/bolt-engine/* fully unauthenticated for asset requests, or (c) configure the coi-serviceworker to skip /api/bolt-engine/* and let the FastAPI proxy's COEP/COOP/CORP headers apply directly.

Because the bridge never installs, the postMessage stream from bolt ('nxt1-bolt-messages') never arrives at the parent — ChatMessages stays in empty state with 'BOOTING ENGINE…' spinner forever. Also no chat history GET / POST is exercised in real usage even though the backend endpoints themselves work. Once the bolt boot bug above is fixed, the rest of the wiring should light up.

integration_issues.flow: Bolt iframe — Vite HMR websocket (pre-existing, iter9 also flagged)
integration_issues.issue: Bolt still attempts wss://.../api/bolt-engine/?token=... (handshake → 403 ingress) and wss://localhost:5173/... (SSL error). Two red console errors per iframe mount. No functional impact but adds noise that masks the real 403s.

integration_issues.flow: Bolt iframe — fetchConfiguredProviders 404 (pre-existing, iter9 also flagged)
integration_issues.issue: GET /api/bolt-engine/api/system/git-info still returns 404 — app/lib/stores/settings.ts logs 'Error fetching configured providers: HTTP 404'. Cosmetic but ANOTHER red error.

action_items: [CRITICAL] Fix bolt iframe boot inside /builder/{id}. Most likely fix: remove `credentialless="true"` from the iframe attribute in /app/frontend/src/pages/BuilderPage.jsx (line ~346). The parent is already COI=true via the coi-serviceworker after first reload, and the iframe is SAME-ORIGIN with the parent — `credentialless` on a same-origin iframe is what's causing module fetches to be sent without credentials and get 403'd. Alternatively, scope-skip /api/bolt-engine/* in /app/frontend/public/coi-serviceworker.js so its fetch handler doesn't strip credentials for those URLs.

[CRITICAL] Verify after fix that window.__nxt1BoltBridge.ready becomes true inside the iframe, bridgeReady fires in BuilderPage, the composer becomes enabled, and posting a message via the bridge results in a chat-history POST (it won't get an actual assistant reply without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, but the user message should round-trip through GET).
[Low, carried over] Silence Vite HMR websocket probe + fetchConfiguredProviders 404 inside bolt-engine sidecar — these add red console errors that masked the real failure during testing.
critical_code_review_comments: BACKEND CHAT PERSISTENCE — VERIFIED GOOD. routes/builder_chat.py is clean: pydantic models, async motor, $setOnInsert + upsert, _id never serialized, role validation, sensible default id (m_<uuid12>) and float ts. All 4 verbs return shapes that match what BuilderPage.jsx consumes. No issues.
BACKEND ROUTER REGISTRATION — VERIFIED. backend/server.py registers builder_chat_router right after bolt_proxy_router as expected.

BOLT-PROXY REGRESSION — VERIFIED. Both /api/bolt-engine/ and /api/bolt-engine/?headless=1 return 200 + COEP credentialless + COOP same-origin (no headless special-case regression). Headers also propagate through the cloudflare ingress.
FRONTEND LAYOUT — VERIFIED. BuilderPage.jsx correctly renders the split (chat left, iframe right) with all required data-testids. Iframe src includes ?headless=1.

FRONTEND HEADLESS CSS — VERIFIED. html[data-nxt1-headless='1'] is set inside the iframe (root.tsx inline script works) and body bg is transparent (the headless block in app/styles/index.scss applied).

FRONTEND BRIDGE PROBE — BROKEN. Chat.client.tsx's bridge install code is never reached because bolt's JS chunks 403 inside the credentialless iframe. Net effect: window.__nxt1BoltBridge is undefined and the composer is disabled forever — see CRITICAL action item above.

POSSIBLE EXTRA: BuilderPage.jsx line ~346 sets `credentialless='true'` on the iframe but the iframe is SAME-ORIGIN as the parent (both served from setup-adjustments.preview.emergentagent.com). Credentialless mode is for CROSS-origin embeds; on a same-origin iframe it just causes sub-resources to lose auth context and the FastAPI proxy then 403s the JS modules. Removing that single attribute is the most likely fix.

success_rate.frontend: 60% — layout + iframe shell pass, but the bolt-iframe boot + bridge install + composer enablement all fail

test_credentials: test@nxt1.local / testpass123 (works, JWT stored as localStorage.nxt1.token)
seed_data_creation: Created/cleaned test chats under TEST_proj_* project ids via the new /api/v1/builder/chat endpoints (deleted at end of each test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_iter10_builder_chat.py — re-run after main agent's fix. The bolt iframe boots fine STANDALONE at /api/bolt-engine/?headless=1 (Workbench + Files + Bolt Terminal all render) — the failure is specific to the iframe-inside-/builder/{id} case. The most informative probe is: iframe.contentWindow.__nxt1BoltBridge (should be an object with keys ready/append/stop/getMessages/getIsLoading/subscribe). Right now it's undefined. Once the bridge installs, also verify the composer enables and a POST /api/v1/builder/chat/{id} fires when the user hits send.
rca of the issue: Bolt iframe in /builder/{id} renders the initial HTML (headless flag + transparent body confirm this) but its module JS chunks 403, so the bolt React tree never mounts. Standalone /api/bolt-engine/?headless=1 works perfectly. Difference between standalone and embedded: the iframe has `credentialless='true'` attribute, and after the first reload the parent page is cross-origin-isolated via the coi-serviceworker. In that combo, credentialless-iframe sub-resource fetches drop the user's session/JWT cookie; the bolt-engine FastAPI proxy then 403s those module requests. Recommended fix: drop `credentialless='true'` from BuilderPage.jsx since the iframe is SAME-ORIGIN with the parent — credentialless is only required for cross-origin embeds under COEP. Verified the symptom by direct comparison (same URL standalone = works; same URL in iframe with credentialless=true = 403s).
